_description: Shared model registry for all AIXIA-workspace projects. Projects resolve model family → current_version at runtime from this file. Maintained by the bi-weekly model-release crawler (scheduled trigger). See ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md §9.5 for policy.
_bootstrap_note: This is the bootstrap state from 2026-04-20. Entries marked bootstrap_unverified: true are seeded from CLAUDE Code context and should be verified on the first crawler run. Pricing drifts; registry values are authoritative — always re-read at call-time.

claude_opus.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change from Jun 15. claude-opus-4-8 at $5/$25 per million remains current flagship and the recommended interim fallback while claude_fable (claude-fable-5) is SUSPENDED. UPDATED Jun 15, 2026: claude-opus-4-7 → claude-opus-4-8 (released May 28, 2026). Pricing unchanged at $5/$25 per million tokens. Fast Mode now 3× cheaper than 4.7 Fast Mode ($10/$50 per million). Key gains: SWE-Bench Pro 69.2% (vs 64.3% on 4.7), OSWorld-Verified 83.4%, ~35% fewer output tokens than 4.7 for equivalent tasks. Available on Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, GitHub Copilot. New tokenizer note from 4.7 still applies: may use 1.0–1.35× more tokens than Opus 4.6 for the same input.

claude_fable.notes: 🚨 API FULLY SUSPENDED since June 12, 2026 — US government export-control directive citing national security. Anthropic enforced a global all-customer shutdown (technically could not filter by nationality in real-time across cloud platforms). Released June 9, suspended 3 days later after US government demonstrated a jailbreak technique; Anthropic disputes that a narrow jailbreak warrants recall of a commercial model at scale. AS OF JUNE 29: Fable 5 (general API) remains fully suspended; Mythos 5 partially restored for ~100 trusted US partner orgs only (cybersecurity/critical infrastructure, inc. Project Glasswing members: Apple, Google, Cisco, Nvidia, Microsoft). Axios (June 27) reports Trump administration expects broad restoration 'this week' — Pentagon and NSA sign-off is the remaining step; prior 'coming days' signals (June 15–16, June 21) have not produced a general restore. AIXIA PROJECTS: All calls to claude-fable-5 will fail until access is restored — route to claude_opus as interim Mythos-class fallback. Check https://www.anthropic.com/news for restoration updates. Original capability: software engineering, agentic coding, knowledge work, vision; $10/$50 per million; prompt caching $1/M cache hits; batch API $5/$25 per million; full 1M context at flat rate.

claude_sonnet.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. claude-sonnet-4-6 at $3/$15 per million remains stable. No Sonnet 4.7/4.8 detected this cycle. Workhorse for user-facing chat and chain synthesis.

claude_haiku.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. Pricing and version stable at $1/$5 per million.

deepseek_general.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. deepseek-v4-flash: 284B total / 13B active params (MoE), 1M context, Thinking+Non-Thinking dual mode. Cache hits at 1/10 standard rate. Compatible with OpenAI ChatCompletions and Anthropic API formats. Chinese-server jurisdiction — disclose in ToS. Router fallback: deepseek → claude_haiku → claude_sonnet. DeepSeek R2 remains unreleased as of this cycle.

deepseek_reasoning.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. Pricing confirmed $0.55/$2.19 (cache hits: $0.14/M input). DeepSeek R2 not yet released. R1 remains ~96% cheaper than OpenAI o1 for comparable tasks. Note: deepseek_v4_pro supports Thinking mode at 1M context if R1's 64K limit is too small.

deepseek_v4_pro.notes: 🚨 PRICING CORRECTION (Jun 29, 2026): The June 1, 2026 crawl INCORRECTLY recorded the promotional discount as expired and set pricing to $1.74/$3.48 standard rate. On May 22, 2026 — nine days BEFORE the May 31 expiry — DeepSeek announced the 75% discount is PERMANENT. Correct pricing is $0.435/$0.87 per million tokens (the 75%-off rate). The 4× price hike warned in the June 1 changelog did NOT occur. Projects that adjusted budgets upward to $1.74/$3.48 should revert immediately. Confirmed by DeepSeek API docs, Engadget, The Next Web, APIdog, and multiple pricing aggregators. Cache hits: $0.003625/M input. 1.6T total / 49B active params (large MoE), 1M context, Thinking+Non-Thinking dual mode.

gemini_pro_multimodal.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; version and pricing unchanged. WATCH: Gemini 3.5 Pro confirmed still in limited Vertex preview as of June 23, 2026 — GA slipped past Sundar Pichai's 'by end of June' target. Features: 2M token context, Deep Think reasoning mode, frontier multimodal. Pricing for 3.5 Pro not yet published. Do NOT update this entry until GA is confirmed. Deprecations completed: gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and gemini-3-pro-image-preview shut down June 25, 2026 as flagged last cycle. Tiered pricing: $2/$12 up to 200K tokens; $4/$18 above 200K. Gemini Pro paid-only (free tier removed April 1, 2026). YouTube URL ingest via file_data.file_uri.

gemini_flash.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change from Jun 15. 🚨 PRICING TRIPLED Jun 15: gemini-3.1-flash → gemini-3.5-flash (GA May 19, 2026). $0.50→$1.50 input, $3.00→$9.00 output per million tokens (200% increase — well above 20% threshold). All AIXIA projects routing bulk text or coding tasks through gemini_flash must re-audit cost models. Context: 1,048,576 tokens. Cached input: $0.15/M (90% discount). Non-global regions: $1.65/$9.90. Batch API: 50% discount. Budget alternative: gemini-3.1-flash-lite still available at lower pricing.

openai_gpt.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; version and pricing unchanged. WATCH: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna previewed June 26, 2026 in LIMITED PREVIEW only (~20 government-vetted orgs, OpenAI API and Codex only — NOT in ChatGPT). Pricing confirmed: Sol $5/$30 per million (1.5M context, max+ultra effort modes, parallel sub-model dispatch); Terra $2.50/$15 (GPT-5.5-competitive, 2× cheaper); Luna $1/$6 (fastest/cheapest). GA expected mid-July 2026 per Axios 'couple weeks' framing; no official date. current_version held at gpt-5.5 until GA. GPT-4.5 removed from ChatGPT June 26 (as flagged). GPT-5.5 Instant remains ChatGPT default. GPT-5.2 deprecated June 12. Fine-tuning API deprecated May 2026 — any AIXIA project on fine-tuned GPT must migrate. GPT-6 confirmed for 2026, no date. Not currently used by AIXIA projects.

openai_reasoning.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. o4-mini outperforms o3-mini at ~50% lower cost. o3 also available ($2.00/M input, 200K context). IMPORTANT: reasoning tokens billed at output rate — effective cost 5–20× listed rate. No o5 release confirmed this cycle. Not currently used by AIXIA projects.

mistral_large.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. Mistral Large 3 2512: 262K context, EU-hosted option for data-residency-sensitive tiers. No new Large model this cycle. Mistral released OCR 4 (document extraction, not a base LM) and new Les Ulis inference facility (Q3 2026 opening) — neither affects registry.

mistral_small.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. Mistral Small 4 (mistral-small-2603): MoE 119B total / 6B active params, 256K context, native multimodal. Cheapest EU-residency multimodal option at $0.15/$0.60.

mistral_medium.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. Mistral Medium 3.5 (April 29, 2026): dense 128B open-weight; unifies instruction-following, reasoning, and coding. 256K context, EU data residency. Output cost ($7.50/M) is 5× mistral_large — only worth it when unified reasoning+coding at long context is required.

mistral_reasoning.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no change. magistral-medium-2506: $2.00/$5.00 per million tokens, 41K context. Budget option: magistral-small-2506 at $0.50/$1.50, 40K context. EU data residency via La Plateforme. Compact context (41K) suits focused reasoning — switch to mistral_medium (256K) when long context is also needed.

llama.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; no open-weight Llama release this cycle. Llama 4 Maverick remains current open-weight offering: 17B active params, 128 experts (400B total), 1M context, natively multimodal MoE. Pricing reflects third-party providers (Together AI, Fireworks, Groq, AWS Bedrock). Also: Llama 4 Scout ($0.15/$0.60, 10M context) for extreme-context tasks; Llama 4 Behemoth (288B active) still not GA. Meta Muse Spark (closed-source, June 2026) is NOT tracked here — not open-weight and not available via standard API routes relevant to AIXIA projects.

grok.notes: Verified Jun 29, 2026; API version and pricing unchanged. grok-4.3 remains the current flagship API model at $1.25/$2.50 per million. V9-Medium (1.5T params, coding-focused, trained on Cursor workflows) launched in consumer Grok product June 16, 2026 but has NOT yet appeared in the xAI API — model string still absent from xAI API docs as of June 29; monitor docs.x.ai. NEW (API): Grok Build 0.1 — dedicated agentic coding model powering the Grok Build CLI — entered public beta via xAI API on May 29, 2026 at $1.00/$2.00 per million tokens; will add as separate registry entry when stable GA. Grok 5 (6T-param MoE, Colossus 2 cluster) still in training. Context window is 1M — projects needing >1M context must use gemini_pro_multimodal. Tiered pricing: requests exceeding 200K total tokens billed at higher rate. Voice STT/TTS APIs live since April 17, 2026.

routing_defaults.description: Canonical default routing for common task categories. Projects override as needed in their local CLAUDE.md.

privacy_mode_routing.description: When a project/tier enables Privacy Mode, all calls route through Claude only (no DeepSeek, no third-party-jurisdiction routes). Studio-tier Echoform users default to Privacy Mode.
